obligations.reason: Debug bus master may access boot ROM without proper authorization, potentially revealing firmware or secrets.
obligations.subject: debug bus master (i_axi_master_dm)
obligations.expected_guard: dmactive asserted, debug authentication (jtag_key) passed, SBA enabled, and address range checking prevents access to protected boot ROM region.
obligations.candidate_path: JTAG → dmi_jtag → dmi_cdc → dm_top → dm_csrs → dm_sba → i_axi_master_dm → AXI interconnect → i_bootrom
state_conditions_to_check: dmactive must be 1
jtag_key comparison result
SBA busy/ready state
address decode and target select for boot ROM
required_evidence_slots: show that debug master transactions can address boot ROM region
confirm absence of hardwired exclusion or access check on debug master's address range
verify whether dmactive or a separate lock controls debug bus access
check for any authentication gating on DMI requests
obligations.uncertainty: Boot ROM content sensitivity and exact address map need confirmation; debug SBA permissions may vary.

obligations.reason: JTAG key (jtag_key) may not be enforced before enabling DMI access, allowing unauthenticated debug control.
obligations.subject: JTAG interface (dmi_jtag)
obligations.expected_guard: jtag_key input must match a stored value before dmi_jtag accepts DMI requests or sets dmactive/dmi_rst_no.
obligations.candidate_path: tck_i, tms_i, td_i → dmi_jtag → jtag_key comparison → dmi_req_valid_o / dmi_rst_no
state_conditions_to_check: jtag_key configuration mechanism (hardwired, fused, writable)
comparison logic location and result gating
testmode_i may bypass key check
reset value of DMI enable state
required_evidence_slots: trace how jtag_key affects dmi_req_valid_o or dmi_rst_no
identify any bypass paths such as testlogic or testmode_i
check if default (unconfigured) key allows access
verify that key check cannot be skipped via JTAG TAP state manipulation
obligations.uncertainty: Exact implementation of jtag_key handling and default enable state unknown from static facts alone.

obligations.reason: Non-debug module reset (ndmreset) from the debug module may reset security-critical lock bits or protected state, undermining write protection.
obligations.subject: debug module (dm_csrs/dm_top)
obligations.object: system reset that clears lock registers or protected state (e.g., CSR lock bits, key valid flags)
obligations.expected_guard: ndmreset must not reset security-critical state that is intended to be persistent, or must be gated by dmactive and proper authorization.
obligations.candidate_path: dm_csrs.ndmreset_o → global ndmreset → reset distribution → csr_regfile / lock block
state_conditions_to_check: dmactive state during ndmreset
reset domain partitioning: which registers are affected by ndmreset vs power-on reset
lock bits in csr_regfile or other modules that should survive ndmreset
required_evidence_slots: list of registers whose reset is driven by ndmreset
show that a security-critical lock is cleared by ndmreset
confirm that debug module can assert ndmreset without additional authorization
check if lock bits can be re-set after ndmreset without proper privilege
obligations.uncertainty: Impact depends on existence of security-critical lock bits and their reset behavior; may be low if no such locks exist.

obligations.reason: Test mode (testmode_i) may expose internal state, bypass debug authentication, or provide an alternative path to protected objects.
obligations.subject: testmode_i signal (multiple modules)
obligations.object: internal debug registers, CSRs, or secret state
obligations.expected_guard: testmode must be deasserted during functional security operation; if active, it must not give direct access to protected state without independent authorization.
obligations.candidate_path: testmode_i → dm_csrs, dmi_jtag_tap, serpent_peripherals → internal state exposure on scan outputs or unguarded reads
state_conditions_to_check: testmode_i active level
logic gating on scan data output (td_o)
effect of testmode on DMI request authorization
testmode impact on lock bits or dmactive
required_evidence_slots: identify which registers or signals become observable when testmode is active
verify that testmode does not override jtag_key or dmactive checks
check for any direct connection from testmode to protected data paths
obligations.uncertainty: Test mode functionality is not detailed; assessment requires RTL analysis of testmode_i connections.

obligations.reason: Crypto keys in serpent_peripherals may be accessible via the debug bus master, leading to secret exposure.
obligations.subject: debug bus master (i_axi_master_dm) or AXI infrastructure
obligations.object: crypto key or internal state registers in serpent_peripherals (e.g., AES key registers accessed via i_apb_to_reg_aes)
obligations.expected_guard: Debug access to crypto registers must be blocked by address exclusion or require explicit authentication; key registers should be write-only or clear-on-read.
obligations.candidate_path: i_axi_master_dm → AXI → APB bridge → i_apb_to_reg_aes → serpent_peripherals internal key registers
state_conditions_to_check: dmactive & SBA enabled
address mapping includes AES peripheral region
read enable gating on key registers
required_evidence_slots: identify key register addresses and attributes
trace bus path from debug master to those addresses
check for any access control (e.g., lock bit) that prevents debug reads
assess whether key data appears on read data bus output
obligations.uncertainty: Presence of crypto keys and exact bus connectivity needs confirmation; serpent_peripherals may not contain keys.

obligations.reason: Debug interface may read/write CPU CSRs without respecting privilege levels, allowing unauthorized control or information leakage.
obligations.subject: debug module (dm_csrs/dm_top) via abstract command or SBA
obligations.object: CPU CSRs (e.g., mstatus, satp, performance counters, debug CSRs)
obligations.expected_guard: Debug access must honor CSR access policies: machine-mode write restrictions, PMP, or require dmactive and proper hart state (halted, debug mode); performance counter lock bits should be respected.
obligations.candidate_path: dm_csrs (hartsel, command) → dm_top → abstract command interface → csr_regfile (csr_addr_i, fu_op, csr_wdata_i, csr_rdata_o)
state_conditions_to_check: dmactive and debug_req state
hart running/halted (resumeack, halted_i)
CSR privilege check logic in csr_regfile (e.g., privilege mode signals, lock bits)
required_evidence_slots: identify CSR write conditions in csr_regfile for debug vs. normal path
check if debug access bypasses privilege checks (e.g., fu_op indicates debug)
verify that lock bits (e.g., PMP lock, counter enable) cannot be cleared by debug
obligations.uncertainty: Exact debug-to-CSR path architecture is not fully specified; some designs isolate debug accesses appropriately.

obligations.reason: Lock/activation bits in dm_csrs (e.g., dmactive, ndmreset enable) may not be sticky, allowing deactivation after being locked.
obligations.subject: dm_csrs register logic
obligations.object: dmactive, ndmreset enable, or other debug lock bits
obligations.expected_guard: Once a lock bit is set (e.g., dmactive lock), it must remain set until a hard reset; writes to clear the lock must be ignored.
obligations.candidate_path: dmi request → dm_csrs → register write logic (case statement at line_start 225 or 298) → lock bit storage
state_conditions_to_check: existence of lock bits in dm_csrs fields
write-enable gating conditions
reset default value of lock bits
required_evidence_slots: identify bits with lock semantics in dm_csrs register definitions
trace write-enable signal for those bits
show a scenario where a locked bit can be cleared via a subsequent DMI write
obligations.uncertainty: Lock bits may not exist; many RISC-V debug implementations do not include such locks. The obligation checks for their presence and effectiveness.
